title: Smart Auto-Connect

tasks.title: Add findBestRotation() function
tasks.description: Create a new function that evaluates all 4 rotations (0, 90, 180, 270) for a given piece type at a given cell. For each rotation, count how many of the piece's connection directions match a neighbor that also has a compatible connection (i.e., neighbor has OPPOSITE[dir] in its connections). Return the rotation with the highest match count. Tie-break: first rotation encountered with the highest score wins. If no rotation has any matches (score 0), return 0 (default).
tasks.location: New function, place it in the UTILITY section after neighborConnected()
tasks.code_changes: Add function findBestRotation(row, col, type) that loops rotations [0,90,180,270], computes connections via getConnections(type, rotation), counts neighbor matches, returns best rotation.

tasks.title: Integrate auto-connect into palette drops only
tasks.description: In the onPointerUp() handler, when a piece is dropped from the palette (dragInfo.source === 'palette') and it's a track piece (not train, not scenery), call findBestRotation() to determine the optimal rotation before calling placePiece(). Grid-source drops must preserve their existing rotation (dragInfo.rotation).
tasks.location: Inside onPointerUp(), in the track piece placement section for palette source
tasks.code_changes: Before calling placePiece() for palette-sourced track pieces, compute rotation via findBestRotation(row, col, dragInfo.type) and pass it to placePiece().

tasks.title: Add green pulse animation on auto-connected edges
tasks.description: When a piece is placed with auto-connect and achieves at least 1 connection, play a brief green pulse animation on the connected edge dots. Add a CSS @keyframes 'connect-pulse' that scales up and glows the connected dots, then returns to normal. Apply the class 'pulsing' to connected dots after placement.
tasks.location: CSS section (after connection-dot styles) and in the placePiece flow
tasks.code_changes: 1. Add @keyframes connect-pulse CSS animation. 2. Add .connection-dot.pulsing class. 3. After auto-connect placement, find connected dots and add 'pulsing' class, remove after animation ends.

tasks.title: Add showAutoConnectPulse() function
tasks.description: Create a function that, given a row/col, finds all .connection-dot.connected elements in that cell and its connected neighbors, adds a 'pulsing' class, and removes it after 600ms. This is called only when auto-connect actually found a rotation with score > 0.
tasks.location: After the placePiece function or in a new VISUAL EFFECTS section
tasks.code_changes: New function showAutoConnectPulse(row, col) that queries connected dots and triggers pulse animation.

acceptance_criteria: AC1: Drop a curve from palette next to a straight track → curve auto-rotates to connect
AC2: Drop a straight from palette between two parallel pieces → connects to both sides
AC3: Manual click-to-rotate still works after placement (rotatePiece unchanged)
AC4: Moving an existing piece from one cell to another preserves its rotation (no auto-rotate on grid-source drag)
AC5: When no neighbors exist, piece drops with default rotation (0°)
AC6: Brief green pulse animation visible on connected dots after auto-connect
AC7: Auto-connect works with all track types: straight, curve, tjunction, crossover, bridge, station
AC8: Random track generator still works correctly (it sets rotations explicitly, not affected by auto-connect)
testing_focus: Drag curve from palette next to straight → verify auto-rotation
Drag straight between two pieces → verify double connection
Click existing piece → rotation still works manually
Drag existing piece from grid → preserves its rotation at new position
Place piece with no neighbors → default rotation 0
Random generate → full loop works, no broken connections
Verify green pulse animation fires and clears
